verses.commentary: Phragelloō (φραγελλόω), Roman flogging. Brutal preliminary to crucifixion. Innocence not respected.
Mock coronation. Thorns: painful crown. Purple: royal mockery. Cruel theater.
Sarcastic homage. Physical assault. Ironic truth in mockery.
Pilate presents Jesus. No grounds for charges. But continues trial.
Idou ho anthrōpos (ἰδοὺ ὁ ἄνθρωπος). Behold the man. Son of Man displayed. Human suffering embodied.
Third innocence declaration. Pilate tries to shift responsibility. Cowardice masquerading as justice.
Blasphemy. Leviticus 24:16. Son of God claim is the issue. Religious grounds for death.
Son of God language terrifies pagan governor. Supernatural dread.
And he went back inside the palace. Where do You come from? But Jesus gave him no answer. Origin question. Silence. No answer given. Isaiah 53:7. Lamb before shearers.
Do You refuse to speak to me? Don't You realize I have power either to free You or to crucify You? Pilate's indignation. Power claim. Authority assertion. Threat.
Power from above. God grants authority. Greater guilt: Caiaphas or Judas. Lesser guilt for Pilate. Degrees of culpability.
Friend of Caesar: technical title. Loss of favor threatened. Jesus as rival to emperor. Treason charged.
Lithostroton (λιθόστρωτος), stone pavement. Gabbatha. Official verdict location. Public judgment.
Day before Passover. Sixth hour. Lambs being slaughtered. Ironic presentation. Your king. True King displayed.

But they shouted, Take Him away! Take Him away! Crucify Him! Shall I crucify your king? We have no king but Caesar. Rejection of kingship. No king but Caesar: tragic apostasy. Israel rejects God's anointed. Rome embraced.
Surrenders to pressure. Jesus delivered. Soldiers take custody. Cross imminent.

Carrying His own cross, He went out to the place of the Skull (in Aramaic called Golgotha). Jesus bears cross. Isaac carrying wood (Genesis 22). Kranion (κρανίον), skull. Golgotha. Execution site outside city.
Crucifixion. Between criminals. Isaiah 53:12. Numbered with transgressors. Central position.
Crime posted. INRI. Jesus Nazarenus Rex Iudaeorum. Truth proclaimed involuntarily.
Hebrew, Latin, Greek. Universal languages. All world reads. Truth broadcast.
Want it disclaimered. Claimed to be. Pilate refuses.
Gegrapha gegrapha (γέγραφα γέγραφα). Perfect tense. Permanent. Irrevocable. Unwitting truth telling.
Four soldiers. Tunic seamless. Valuable garment. High priestly robe echoes (Exodus 28:32).

Let's not tear it. Let's decide by lot who will get it. This happened that the scripture might be fulfilled that said, They divided My clothes among them and cast lots for My garment. So this is what the soldiers did. Gambling for garment. Psalm 22:18 fulfilled. Scripture details prophesied. Providence in smallest acts.
Mary, mother of Jesus. Others present. Faithful witnesses. Men fled; women remain.
Beloved disciple (John). New relationship created. Maternal care.
John receives Mary. Immediate obedience. Household provision. Honor to Mary.
Scripture fulfillment continues. Psalm 69:21. Physical suffering. Human thirst.
Hyssop: Passover plant (Exodus 12:22). Symbolic connection to sacrifice.
Perfect tense. Completed. Accomplished. Paid in full. Voluntary death. Spirit released. Mission completed.
Passover Sabbath. Deuteronomy 21:23. Bodies must not remain. Leg-breaking hastens death.
Legs broken. Death accelerated.
No need to break. Passover lamb unblemished (Exodus 12:46).
Confirmation of death. Blood and water. Medical accuracy. Physical and spiritual significance. Cleansing and life.
John's testimony. Truth claim. Belief purpose. Apologetic intent.
Passover lamb typology. No broken bones. Prophetic fulfillment.
Piercing prophesied. Looking brings repentance. Future recognition.
Fear. Now courage. Requests body. Pilate grants. Burial begins.
John 3 connection. Night visitor now day confessor. Royal burial spices. Lavish amount. Honor to King.
Linen and spices. Proper honor. Hasty but dignified.
Near Golgotha. New tomb. Unused. Honor and purity.
Preparation day. Nearby tomb. Convenient. Providential location. Sabbath approaching.
